name: Realtime Alpha 101
author: Timeplus
description: Streaming implementation of ten WorldQuant alphas (#1, #2, #3, #4, #6, #9, #12, #22, #41, #54) over a synthetic multi-stock random feed. Demonstrates tumble-window bars, lag/lags arrays, cross-sectional rank, rolling correlation, and per-alpha backtest views in Timeplus streaming SQL.

config.description: Tumble-window bar interval. Smaller = more real-time but more CPU; larger = smoother bars but slower alpha warm-up (1s → ~25s, 5s → ~125s, 1m → ~25min).

Number of simulated stocks (2–10). Cross-sectional rank with `(rank − 1) / (N − 1) − 0.5` produces a mean-zero alpha in [−0.5, +0.5]. Default 3 is a good starting point; ceiling is 10 (limited by the built-in per-stock base-price seed array). N=1 yields null alpha (no peers to rank against).

How the alpha signal maps to a position weight in the backtest: `linear` uses alpha_prev directly (continuous weighting, magnitude matters); `sign` uses sign(alpha_prev) (equal-magnitude long/short, direction only — robust to alpha-noise outliers).

dashboards.name: Realtime Alpha 101
dashboards.description: Live prices, volume and alpha leaderboard — pick the alpha via the dropdown

dashboards.name: Alpha 101 Backtest
dashboards.description: Cumulative PnL, Sharpe and hit rate — pick the alpha via the dropdown

name: AWS Resource Cost Monitor
author: Timeplus
description: Realtime AWS resource usage and cost monitoring. Polls EC2 instances, EBS volumes, and S3 buckets across configured regions, joins live inventory with AWS On-Demand pricing, and breaks down hourly spend by service, region, and creator. Requires an IAM principal with: ec2:DescribeInstances, ec2:DescribeVolumes, s3:ListAllMyBuckets, cloudwatch:GetMetricStatistics, cloudtrail:LookupEvents, pricing:GetProducts.

config.description: AWS access key ID for the IAM principal used to poll resources.
AWS secret access key.
AWS regions to monitor.
Which AWS services to monitor.
How often to re-scan resources, in seconds.
How often to re-fetch the AWS Pricing API.

dashboards.name: AWS Cost Monitor
dashboards.description: Realtime AWS spend by service, region, and creator

name: Bluesky Social Analytics
author: Timeplus
description: Real-time social media analytics from Bluesky's Jetstream firehose — post volume, engagement metrics, language breakdown, top posters, and a live activity feed.

config.description: Bluesky Jetstream WebSocket endpoint URL
AT Protocol collection NSIDs to subscribe to. Examples: app.bsky.feed.post, app.bsky.feed.like, app.bsky.graph.follow, app.bsky.feed.repost

dashboards.name: Bluesky Social Analytics
dashboards.description: Real-time post volume, engagement, language breakdown, and live feed

name: Complex Event Processing Demo
author: Timeplus
description: Complex event processing demo with SQL-based fraud detection and JavaScript UDF pattern matching over simulated random event streams.

name: Cisco ASA DDoS Detection Demo
author: Timeplus

config.description: HTTP endpoint that receives DDoS alert POSTs. Payload: {"title": "...", "message": "...", "severity": "high"}.
Spike ratio multiplier that triggers an alert. An IP is flagged when its live traffic exceeds this multiple of its baseline.
Source IP used by the simulated attacker for baseline and attack traffic.
Events per second for the background traffic generator (many random IPs).
Events per second for the attacker IP normal baseline traffic.
Events per second for the attack traffic burst (used when attack MV is resumed).
Stream TTL in hours. Data older than this is automatically dropped.
Maximum logstore size per stream in bytes (default ~100 MB).
Maximum logstore retention in milliseconds (default 5 minutes).

dashboards.name: Cisco ASA DDoS Detection
dashboards.description: Real-time spike ratio monitoring, baseline tables, and live traffic

name: Real-time Machine Learning Feature Pipeline for Game Analytics Demo
author: Timeplus
description: Demo of real-time ML feature pipeline for game analytics — player action features, transaction features, social engagement features, and composite feature views for player behaviour analysis and spend anomaly detection.

config.description: Logstore retention size in bytes for all streams (default 100 MB)
Logstore retention duration in milliseconds for all streams (default 5 min)
Retention period in hours for all event and feature streams
Events per second for the player actions random stream
Events per second for the transactions random stream
Events per second for the performance metrics random stream
Slack webhook URL for spend-anomaly alerts (leave blank to disable)
Total-spend threshold (USD) across last 10 transactions that triggers an alert

name: Geo IP Lookup
author: Timeplus
description: Interactive IPv4 to city geo lookup powered by the dbip IPv4 dataset. Loads ~3M ranges into an IP_TRIE dictionary for sub-millisecond dict_get lookups. Includes a dashboard with text input, lookup result table, and map.

config.description: Timeplus user that the IP_TRIE dictionary connects as to read its source stream. The app creates this user at install via a type:system resource. Override only if you need a different name.
Password for dict_user. Used in both CREATE USER and the dictionary source.

dashboards.name: Geo IP Lookup
dashboards.description: Type an IP, see the city.

name: GitHub Activity Monitor
author: Timeplus
description: Real-time GitHub public events pipeline. Streams public events from the GitHub API into Timeplus for live analytics.

config.description: GitHub personal access token (PAT) with public_repo scope

dashboards.name: GitHub Events Dashboard
dashboards.description: Real-time view of GitHub public events

name: Hacker News Live Feed
author: Timeplus
description: Continuously ingests Hacker News posts and comments via the official Firebase API into a Timeplus stream, ready for real-time analysis of trending stories, active users, and post-type distributions.

config.description: Retention period in days for the hn_post stream
Logstore retention size in bytes for the hn_post stream (default 100 MB)
Logstore retention duration in milliseconds for the hn_post stream (default 5 min)
How often the ingestion task runs (e.g. 10s, 1m, 5m)
Maximum runtime per task execution before it is aborted (e.g. 30s, 1m)
Number of recent items to look back on the first run (before any post is stored)
Maximum number of new items to fetch per task execution
Base URL of the OpenAI-compatible API used for embeddings and chat
API key for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint
Embedding model used to vectorize stories and questions
Chat model used to answer questions

dashboards.name: Hacker News RAG Q&A
dashboards.description: Ask questions over live HN stories with vector search + LLM, plus embedding-pipeline health

name: Real-time Investment Insights
author: Timeplus
description: Real-time trading monitoring demo — order management, position tracking, market data, continuous auction participation rate, and live P&L. Data is generated internally via random streams; no external Kafka required.

config.description: Events per second for the order random stream
Events per second for the quote (stock price) random stream
Retention period in hours for exchange_order and execution streams
How often (in hours) the pre_value task recalculates portfolio baseline
Timeout (in minutes) for each pre_value task run
Logstore retention size in bytes for all streams (default 100 MB)
Logstore retention duration in milliseconds for all streams (default 5 min)

dashboards.name: Investment Insights Dashboard

name: Coinbase Crypto Analytics
author: Timeplus
description: Real-time crypto market data from Coinbase WebSocket — OHLC candlesticks, RSI, order book snapshots, VWAP, and quantitative alpha signals.

config.description: Coinbase WebSocket feed URL

Coinbase product IDs to subscribe to. Find valid product IDs at https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/exchange/reference/exchangerestapi_getproducts or by running: curl https://api.exchange.coinbase.com/products | jq '[.[].id]' Examples: "BTC-USD", "ETH-USD", "SOL-USD", "XRP-USD", "DOGE-USD"

dashboards.name: Coinbase Market Data
dashboards.description: Real-time OHLC, returns, and RSI for Coinbase crypto pairs

name: OCSF Security Event Analytics
author: Timeplus
description: Real-time OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework) event analytics. Generates synthetic security events (Authentication, Network, Process, Security Finding) via the timeplus-ocsf-simulator, flattens nested JSON into typed views, and surfaces threat-detection panels in a dashboard.

config.description: Comma-separated OCSF class UIDs to generate. Defaults to all four classes flattened by this app: Process (1007), Security Finding (2001), Authentication (3002), Network Activity (4001).
Seconds between events emitted by the simulator (parsed as float). The function is a per-event iterator; 0.1 = ~10 events/sec.
OCSF schema version to generate.
TTL for the ocsf_events stream in hours.

dashboards.name: OCSF Overview
dashboards.description: Headline counters across all four event classes

dashboards.name: OCSF — Authentication
dashboards.description: Authentication (class 3002) — failures, brute force, top sources

dashboards.name: OCSF — Network Activity
dashboards.description: Network (class 4001) — protocol traffic, high-severity events, scanning

dashboards.name: OCSF — Process Activity
dashboards.description: Process (class 1007) — creates, suspicious command lines, privilege mismatch

dashboards.name: OCSF — Security Findings
dashboards.description: Findings (class 2001) — severity trends, malware, top finding titles

name: NYC Taxi Fleet Analytics
author: Timeplus
description: Real-time NYC taxi fleet analytics powered by the timeplus-taxi-simulator Python package — live map and fleet speed KPIs.

config.description: Number of simulated taxis (1-500 recommended).
Base vehicle speed in km/h. Each car gets +/-20% variation.

dashboards.name: Taxi Fleet
dashboards.description: Live map, speed analytics, and fleet KPIs
